customer_anxiety_profile: Low anxiety, cooperative posture — but the call ended before any appointment structure was established. The customer is not resistant; she is simply waiting for a follow-up that has no locked time. Risk is drift and forgetting, not active resistance.
unresolved_concerns: Bill not captured — Ernest (primary occupant, bill-holder) was unreachable; his mailbox was full
No locked callback time — rep said 'call you back whenever,' leaving follow-up entirely open-ended
Three co-owners on title (Ernest + two sisters) — none confirmed for appointment inclusion
Husband's role as financial manager of the property not clarified — he provided the bill estimate but his decision-making role is undefined
sensitive_topics_raised: Mother recently deceased — customer disclosed this mid-call while searching for the bill; the property appears to have been the mother's home or connected to her estate
Multiple family members managing the property's paperwork — situation is administratively complex post-bereavement
exact_promises_rep_made: Sent a text to the customer's phone with instructions to send a photo of the utility bill's yearly kilowatt usage graph
Confirmed the customer's email is on file
Promised to call back and schedule once the bill photo is received
closer_should_know: This is a three-owner townhouse (Ernest + two sisters) where Ernest is the primary occupant and bill-holder — he was unreachable during the setter call. The mother recently passed away and the property paperwork is in transition. The setter left a soft callback with no locked time; the closer should treat this as a warm but unanchored lead that needs a locked appointment time and Ernest's inclusion before it can be a reliable sit.
